grounded_note: AI proposal verbatim at T32 (digest line 451) matches: spec/feature-issue in .scratch OR a dump-validation script for userSelectedFolders before deciding the plan. User response ' 能加进去吗?' at T33 (line 457) matches exactly. Quotes are faithful (curly-quote substitution only).
leg1_note: Two concrete named options (spec/feature-issue in .scratch/, dump script targeting userSelectedFolders). Specific and identifiable.
leg2_note: User did not pick up either preliminary option; 'can it be added?' pulled toward implementation feasibility.
leg3_note: The proposal's AXIS was adding Cowork support; the two options were merely PRELIMINARY/process steps (write a spec first, or validate first) before that same implementation. The subsequent work (T44 plan, T45 user ' 那你加上去吧 ', T49-T80 edits to work-log-synthesizer.md/README/CHANGELOG) IS the Cowork implementation the research was building toward. Declining 'spec first / validate first' and going straight to implementing the same feature is process-skip / normal steering, not a genuinely DIFFERENT axis. Lens spec ❌ bullet: 'User explored AI's proposal and then steered it → engagement, not negative-space.'
genuine_injection_note: Same goal both sides; no orthogonal new direction injected, only skipping the proposed pre-work.
refutation: Grounded and legs 1-2 hold, but leg3 fails: the 'spec-or-validate-first' proposal and the subsequent direct implementation share one axis — adding Cowork support (T44-T80). User merely skipped the proposed preliminary steps; this is steering/process-skip, not a macro pivot to a different axis.

grounded_note: AI three-option proposal verbatim at T85 (digest lines 1481-1484): run /work-memory:work-report; keep/delete mock-user/mock-org/agent-production test project; commit on a new branch. User response 'ok 你本地启动 我找个 cowork 的目录试试效果' at T86 (line 995) matches.
leg2_note: User addressed none of the three; redirected to 'start it locally so I can try a cowork dir.'
leg3_note: Subsequent work (T87-T95 build + smoke-test, leading to launching the host) is preparing/launching the app for user self-testing — a different axis from work-report projection, test-project cleanup, or committing. None of the three was executed in-session.
genuine_injection_note: User injected a new direction (launch locally for hands-on trial) orthogonal to the wrap-up trio.
refutation: Cannot refute: quotes verbatim and accurate at T85/T86; all three proposed wrap-up actions were dropped and the conversation pivoted to building/launching for user self-test (T87-T95, culminating T137-T138). Genuine negative-space.

grounded_note: AI's user-run terminal procedure verbatim at T102 (digest lines 1215-1227): cd into AgentProduction, claude --plugin-dir, then /work-memory:work-log-init and /work-memory:work-log; AgentProduction named as recommended dir. User response '你直接启动客户端吧' at T103 (line 1245) matches.
leg1_note: Specific multi-step procedure with named commands and named target dir.
leg2_note: User declined to run the procedure themselves, asking the AI to launch instead.
leg3_note: EXECUTOR-SWAP, not a different axis. The WORK is identical — run the work-log synthesizer against AgentProduction (the AI's own recommended target). T104-T114 has the AI init AgentProduction and run '/work-memory:work-log' headless (T114) — i.e. it executed the very same /work-log step it had told the user to run, just headlessly instead of in a user terminal. Lens spec explicitly warns against 'executor-swap dramatized as a pivot.' Same axis (validate Cowork synthesizer on AgentProduction), only who-executes changed.
genuine_injection_note: No new direction injected; user said 'you launch it directly' = transfer execution, same task.
refutation: Leg3 fails: the AI's post-T103 work (T107 init AgentProduction, T114 'claude -p /work-memory:work-log') IS the same procedure it proposed at T102 — same target dir, same /work-log step — merely executed headlessly by the AI instead of by the user in a terminal. That is an executor-swap, which the lens spec explicitly excludes, not a different-axis pivot.
